scope_sets.rationale: The current roadmap slice spans both read-side and write-side MTD Income Tax operations, so the interactive HMRC session requests only the two self-assessment scopes already published by the subscribed APIs and nothing wider.

source_refs.rationale: HMRC user-restricted guidance defines space-delimited scope requests and the sandbox authorisation/token endpoints.
The current Business Details OAS publishes the read/write self-assessment scope pair.

callback_bindings.initiation_surface: Browser shells start the HMRC journey, but the callback lands on the gateway-owned HTTPS redirect endpoint.
callback_bindings.post_authorization_return_strategy: Gateway completes token exchange, rehydrates the server session, and redirects back to the owning browser shell or object route.

source_refs.rationale: HMRC redirect URI budget, HTTPS rule for web callbacks, and fragment/IP-address prohibitions.

callback_bindings.initiation_surface: Native macOS app opens the system browser and receives the return on the environment-bound loopback path.
callback_bindings.post_authorization_return_strategy: Local callback server hands the code and opaque return context back to the governed gateway, which completes token exchange before resuming the native scene.

callback_bindings.initiation_surface: Browser shells start the HMRC journey, but the callback lands on the gateway-owned HTTPS redirect endpoint.
callback_bindings.post_authorization_return_strategy: Gateway completes token exchange, rehydrates the server session, and redirects back to the owning browser shell or object route.

source_refs.rationale: Pre-production must mirror production-like policy without sharing production provider trust.

callback_bindings.initiation_surface: Native macOS app opens the system browser and receives the return on the environment-bound loopback path.
callback_bindings.post_authorization_return_strategy: Local callback server hands the code and opaque return context back to the governed gateway, which completes token exchange before resuming the native scene.

source_refs.rationale: Native operator flows stay system-browser managed while server truth remains authoritative.

operation_bindings.rationale: Reference-data reads stay on the shared current-slice HMRC scope set so sessions are not widened beyond read/write self-assessment.

source_refs.rationale: Business Details is the reference-data source for this operation family.

operation_bindings.rationale: Obligation retrieval remains within the same interactive scope set because the current slice also includes write-capable submission work.

source_refs.rationale: Obligations is the source API for this operation family.

operation_bindings.rationale: Calculation retrieval shares the same two-scope set as the write-capable calculation journey for the current slice.

source_refs.rationale: Individual Calculations is the source API for this operation family.

operation_bindings.rationale: Triggering a calculation is a write-capable operation and therefore remains within the shared current-slice read/write set.

source_refs.rationale: Individual Calculations is the source API for this operation family.

operation_bindings.rationale: Final declaration is a write-capable operation and remains bound to the same current-slice scope set.

source_refs.rationale: Individual Calculations is the source API for this operation family.

operation_bindings.rationale: Status reconciliation stays on the same current-slice scope set because it is inseparable from write-capable calculation and submission flows.

source_refs.rationale: Individual Calculations is the source API for this operation family.

operation_bindings.rationale: Quarterly update submission spans both self-employment and property APIs and therefore stays on the shared two-scope set.

source_refs.rationale: Self Employment Business is a source API for periodic submissions.
Property Business is a source API for periodic submissions.

operation_bindings.rationale: Post-finalisation amendments remain within the same current-slice read/write scope pair as the quarterly submission APIs.

source_refs.rationale: Self Employment Business is a source API for amendment submissions.
Property Business is a source API for amendment submissions.

typed_gaps: HMRC's user-restricted guidance currently documents sandbox authorisation at https://test-www.tax.service.gov.uk/oauth/authorize and token exchange at https://test-api.service.hmrc.gov.uk/oauth/token, while current API OAS files still publish https://api.service.hmrc.gov.uk/oauth/authorize and /oauth/token in their security schemes. Taxat follows the user-restricted guidance for sandbox runtime endpoint selection and keeps the discrepancy explicit.
notes: Browser-originated HMRC requests always return through the gateway-owned HTTPS callback.
Native macOS flows may receive the code on localhost, but the browser and the device are never the long-term token custody boundary.
